name: dag-field-presence
claim: Field presence on a DAG node is determined by structural type; compounds and inferences require deps, non-contract directives require deps or a stipulation artifact, declared contracts may be standalone, and contract fields are directive-only

rules.given: A node of any type
rules.then: id, type, kind, domain, tags, claim, subjects, status, created are all valid fields

rules.given: A node has type: primitive
rules.then: artifact (required) and evidence (dated, append-only) are valid; deps, materialized, rules, invariants are not meaningful

rules.given: A node has type: compound
rules.then: The node must have a non-empty deps array - a compound by definition composes multiple deps

rules.given: A node has type: inference
rules.then: The node must have a non-empty deps array - a conclusion traces its grounding even when its claim exceeds it

rules.given: A node has type: directive and does not carry rules or invariants
rules.scenario: Non-contract directive grounds in deps or stipulation
rules.then: The node must have non-empty deps or a stipulation artifact (plan:/user:/session:/document:) - adoption is grounded in beliefs or in the record of the adoption event

rules.given: A node has type: directive and carries non-empty rules or invariants
rules.then: The node may have empty deps - contracts can be declared (formalize policy standalone) or derived; both forms are valid

invariants: Compounds cannot have empty deps
Inferences cannot have empty deps
Active non-contract directives carry deps or a stipulation artifact
Contract fields (contract, rules, invariants) are directive-only
Fields are emitted in a stable schema-defined order
Fields absent in the source JSON are not emitted on round-trip (presence tracked via _keys)
tags defaults to empty list when absent from source

evidence.detail: Accepted via human adjudication against cb:c027. Reasoning: cb-schema-v2: deps requirements re-attach per type (compounds, inferences, non-contract directives under the D4 grounding rule); contract fields are directive-only. The stale 'confidence' shared-field reference is dropped in passing.

name: dag-status-lifecycle
claim: DAG node status follows a directed transition: active -> superseded | retracted | retired, with all non-active states terminal and requiring the appropriate linkage fields; retired is the directive-only exit - a withdrawn rule, never a falsified claim
invariants: status is a closed enum: active | superseded | retracted | retired
superseded_by is non-null iff status is superseded
retracted_on and retracted_reason are non-null iff status is retracted
retired status is exclusive to directives - descriptive claims are superseded or retracted, never retired; an inference is falsified, a directive is violated or withdrawn
terminal states (superseded, retracted, retired) have no outgoing transitions and are immutable except for appending to evidence

evidence.detail: Accepted via human adjudication against cb:c029. Reasoning: cb-schema-v2: retired widens from contract-grade implications to any directive (a withdrawn rule retires whether or not it carries machine-checkable teeth); the contract-field transition requirement drops accordingly. Direction-of-fit grounding via cb:a471.

name: contract-identity
claim: A node is contract-grade iff its type is directive and its rules or invariants array is non-empty - contract is the machine-checkable grade of a directive, not a type. The c-prefix ID convention is a naming reflection of this structural property, not the definition of contract identity. Code that operates on contracts must detect them via Belief.contract?/1 and never by ID prefix matching.

rules.given: An arbitrary DAG node
rules.then: The function returns true iff the node has non-empty rules or non-empty invariants

rules.given: A new contract-grade directive is being assigned an ID
rules.then: The ID should use the c-prefix convention (c001, c002, ...) to flag the contract nature to human readers

rules.given: A primitive, compound, or inference is being edited to add rules
rules.scenario: Contract structure cannot be retrofitted to descriptive types
rules.then: The write is rejected - contract grade exists only on directives; mint a directive that deps on the descriptive node instead

invariants: Belief.contract?/1 is the canonical check for contract identity
Contract-grade directives may use retired status (per the status lifecycle)
c-prefix is a convention, not a structural guarantee - always check rules/invariants, not the ID

evidence.detail: Accepted via human adjudication against cb:c031. Reasoning: cb-schema-v2: contract-grade re-attaches to the directive type (c031's biconditional preserved verbatim); the retired-status invariant rewords per the c029 successor's widening.

name: conflict-scope-definition
claim: Two active directives share conflict scope when they occupy overlapping territory along at least one axis (tag, subject ref, subject type) within the same domain - overlap signals the pair is close enough that a contradiction would be meaningful. Contradictory prescriptions are actionable conflicts; contradictory inferences are dissent and live outside this audit's scope.

rules.given: Two active directives
rules.then: They overlap only if they are in the same domain or both have no domain; distinct non-nil domains never overlap

rules.given: Two active directives in the same domain
rules.then: They overlap if they share a tag, share a subject ref, share a subject type, or both carry empty tags and subjects (domain-global)

rules.given: A pair flagged by the audit
rules.then: The audit surfaces the candidate pair; whether the pair actually contradicts, is redundant, or is compatible requires reviewer judgment

invariants: Overlap is symmetric - overlap(a,b) iff overlap(b,a)
A node does not overlap with itself
Cross-domain pairs with distinct non-nil domains never overlap
Overlap is a necessary but not sufficient condition for contradiction
The audit surfaces candidates; resolution requires reviewer judgment

evidence.detail: Accepted via human adjudication against cb:c032. Reasoning: cb-schema-v2: conflict scope re-attaches to active directives - the audit is response-to-violation machinery and a theory cannot be violated. Inference-conflict (structured dissent) is consensus-thread work, explicitly out of scope.

claim: Schema discipline: belief provenance is carried by an artifact field; contract-grade directives carry contract:true with non-empty rules/invariants; the implication field is absent; enum-shaped fields (kind, domain, artifact-scheme) take values from c039/c041/c043 respectively; kind binds allowed structural types via the kind-type derivation table.
rules: The artifact field is the canonical provenance carrier on beliefs. Its value is null or a string matching scheme:id where scheme is declared by c043.
The kind field's value is null or one of the values declared by c039.
The domain field's value is null or one of the values declared by c041.
The contract field is a boolean. It is allowed only on type: directive.
contract: true is required iff rules or invariants are non-empty (bidirectional).
The implication field is absent on all beliefs.
Beliefs with kind: action-item have type: directive, contract: false, and empty rules/invariants.
The four structural types are distinct and are not merged: a compound states exactly what its deps jointly state, an inference may exceed them, a directive prescribes.
Artifacts (external documents, threads, files) are not promoted to belief nodes; they are referenced via the artifact field only.
invariants: For all beliefs b: b.contract == true implies b.type == 'directive'.
For all beliefs b: ((b.rules // []) ++ (b.invariants // [])) non-empty iff b.contract == true.
For all beliefs b: b.artifact == null OR b.artifact matches /^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*:.+/.
For all beliefs b: b.kind == null OR b.kind is in c039.enum.
For all beliefs b: b.artifact == null OR scheme(b.artifact) is in c043.enum.
For all beliefs b: b.domain == null OR b.domain is in c041.enum.
For all beliefs b: b does not have an `implication` field.
For all beliefs b with b.kind == 'action-item': b.type == 'directive' AND b.contract != true AND (b.rules // []) is empty AND (b.invariants // []) is empty.

evidence.detail: Accepted via human adjudication against cb:c038. Reasoning: cb-schema-v2: schema discipline restated for four types; contract attaches to directive; the stale c040 scheme-enum reference is corrected to its successor c043; the kind-type table joins the enum family.

name: kind-type-table
claim: Kind binds mood: each belief kind maps to the structural types it may inhabit, converting the prescriptive/descriptive boundary from prose to a deterministic check. Prescriptive kinds bind to directive only; descriptive kinds may never be directives; dual kinds (definition, schema) carry both moods and the choice is per belief by direction of fit.
invariants: For all active beliefs b with kind k present in this table: b.type is in row(k).allowed_types
Prescriptive kinds never appear on descriptive types; descriptive kinds never appear on directives
A kind absent from the table is unchecked, not failed - the kind enum is the closure authority

evidence.detail: The mood half of the v2 type function (type = f(mood(kind), grounding, scope)), landed with the c026-family supersessions. Bindings per design.md's three groups over the 38 c039 kinds. Preflight review per the a467/c040 precedent recorded on the plan-0 primitives this contract rests on.

name: subject-containment
claim: A conjunction cannot be about something its parts are not about: an active compound's subject refs must be a subset of the union of its deps' subject refs. Scope widening is the structural signature of inference - a claim whose subjects escape its deps is an inference or carries commentary to trim, never a compound. Empty subjects pass vacuously; the check earns its discriminating power only where subjects are populated.

rules.given: An active compound with non-empty subjects
rules.then: Every subject ref appears in the union of its deps' subject refs

rules.given: A compound whose subjects escape the dep union
rules.then: The node is re-typed as an inference, or its claim is trimmed to the conjunction and the widened content minted as an inference depping on it

rules.given: A compound with a dep outside the loaded belief set
rules.then: The compound is skipped, not failed - the union cannot be computed without the dep's subjects

invariants: For all active compounds c with subjects: subject_refs(c) is a subset of the union of subject_refs over deps(c)
A compound with empty subjects passes vacuously
Containment is checked on refs; subject types classify and do not partition scope

evidence.detail: The scope half of the v2 type function, with the strict-aggregate doctrine (D3, cb:a474) as its authoring-side companion. Deterministic verifier check; subject-sparse graphs triage compounds by judgment instead.

name: directive-grounding
claim: A prescription is adopted: an active non-contract directive grounds in deps (the beliefs it rests on) or in a stipulation artifact citing plan:, user:, session:, or document: - the record of the adoption event. Contract-grade directives may be declared standalone from policy. External-source schemes never ground a directive.

rules.given: An active non-contract directive with non-empty deps
rules.then: The directive is grounded - it rests on the beliefs it cites

rules.given: An active non-contract directive with empty deps
rules.then: The artifact must carry a stipulation scheme (plan:, user:, session:, document:) recording where the prescription was adopted

rules.given: A contract-grade directive
rules.then: Both deps and stipulation artifact may be absent - contracts can be declared from policy

invariants: For all active non-contract directives d: deps(d) non-empty OR scheme(d.artifact) in {plan, user, session, document}
Stipulation schemes are a closed set: plan | user | session | document
External-source schemes (source, https, gmail) never ground a directive

evidence.detail: D4 confirmed, with the execution amendment admitting document: - the design's own worked re-homes (method:a1-a5, the lib: rules) are document-grounded stipulations. Legitimizes conventions as directives without manufacturing fake deps.

claim: Active behavioral directives are not cached to a digest file; load them live from the graph by querying active directives (`mix bs list directive`). Per cb:a386, a digest whose freshness depends on remembering to regenerate it is an antipattern that embeds the staleness it was meant to solve - render live from the DAG instead.

evidence.detail: Accepted via human adjudication against cb:a445. Reasoning: cb-schema-v2: the query token changes with the type rename (bs list implication -> bs list directive); content otherwise unchanged.

claim: The mechanism is a directed acyclic graph of beliefs with four structural types: primitive (what a single source said), compound (what its deps jointly state), inference (a conclusion licensed to exceed its deps), and directive (what should happen or must hold - a prescription the house stands behind).

evidence.detail: Accepted via human adjudication against cb:a446. Reasoning: cb-schema-v2: the CLAUDE.md architecture sentence states four types with their one-line definitions from the design's summary table.

claim: Immutability makes change traceable: a belief is never edited in place; a changed belief is superseded by a new one and linked forward. Composition makes the whole greater than its parts - an inference concludes what none of its inputs states alone, and pays for that licence by being falsifiable on its own, independent of its deps.

evidence.detail: Accepted via human adjudication against cb:a447. Reasoning: cb-schema-v2 D3: the concludes-beyond-inputs sentence belongs to the inference type; a compound states exactly what its deps jointly state (strict-aggregate doctrine, cb:a474).

claim: Skills coordinate authoring, query, and presentation: `/assert` adds beliefs from artifacts, entities, or reasoning; `/assert-session` persists session rules and agent error patterns; `/assertions` queries and traverses the graph; `/materialize` turns directives into concrete tasks; `/present-codepath` walks a codepath - a code-anchored collection rendered as a narrated, branching tour of real source that can also run as a test suite (`mix cb.verify.codepath`).

evidence.detail: Accepted via human adjudication against cb:a469. Reasoning: cb-schema-v2: /materialize operates on directives (you do not materialize a theory).

claim: Schema rules in force: no `confidence` field; no `patch` kind; no separate `implication` prose field, since meaning is carried by `claim` plus `deps`; primitives ground their claim with an `artifact` URI and dated `evidence`; non-contract directives ground in deps or a stipulation artifact; and `contract: true` is biconditional with non-empty `rules`/`invariants` (per c056).

evidence.detail: CLAUDE.md schema-rules sentence updated for v2: the c038 reference moves to its successor c056 and the D4 grounding rule joins the in-force list.
Accepted via human adjudication against cb:a452. Reasoning: cb-schema-v2: the referenced schema-discipline contract id changes with the c038 supersession; the grounding rule joins the rules in force.

claim: The schema's single source of truth is `lib/cb/belief.ex`. The graph's own schema is expressed as contracts in the graph: c051 (the four structural types), c053 (status lifecycle and immutability), c056 (schema discipline), c039/c043/c041 (the closed kind/artifact-scheme/domain enums), c057 (the kind-type table), c058 (subject containment), c059 (directive grounding), and c055 (conflict scope). Read one with `mix bs show cb:c051`.

evidence.detail: Accepted via human adjudication against cb:a468. Reasoning: cb-schema-v2: the contract id map regenerates after the c026-family supersessions; the three new v2 contracts join the family.

claim: The framework CLAUDE.md compiles from the beliefs listed in this contract's render_sections. The file is read-only and regenerated from the cb: graph by `mix cb.generate.claude_md`. Authoring happens by creating beliefs (via /assert) and by editing this contract's render_sections; hand-edits to CLAUDE.md are overwritten on the next generation.

rules.header_comment:
<!-- Generated by mix cb.generate.claude_md from the cb: DAG. Do not edit; run the task to regenerate. -->

# Composable Beliefs

A framework for giving AI agents persistent, source-grounded, inspectable reasoning that survives session boundaries and composes into understanding the agent never explicitly derived.

invariants: CLAUDE.md is read-only - edits are overwritten on next generation
Every line of output traces to exactly one belief's claim field
deps equals the union of all beliefs referenced in render_sections
Section titles are human-readable; tags remain free for cross-cutting audit queries

evidence.detail: Accepted via human adjudication against cb:c048. Reasoning: cb-schema-v2: render_sections re-point to the v2 successors of the six superseded CLAUDE.md render beliefs (a445/a446/a447/a452/a468/a469 -> a477-a482); the contract's own claim and rules shape are unchanged - editing render_sections is this contract's documented authoring path, done by supersession.

claim: The cb ecosystem migrated to the four-type v2 schema on 2026-06-10: the framework graph (76 nodes re-typed, the c026/c027/c029/c031/c032/c038 contract family superseded by c051-c056, new contracts c057/c058/c059, CLAUDE.md render beliefs superseded and regenerated) and all seven sibling collections (codepath, method, sdl, toy, lib, agent-behavior, paradigm) via mix cb.migrate.v2, with adjudicated splits sdl:a006 -> a008+a009, toy:a9 -> a10+a11, the D3 trim sdl:a3 -> a010, and six agent-behavior mood re-homes (a396-a401). All eight collections verify under v2; sdl reproduces exactly its two teaching method-check failures.

evidence.detail: Executed under Mark's proceed authorization following D1-D6 resolution (D1 'inference' confirmed by Mark for launch, overriding the immutable-history consideration). Execution amendments recorded in the design doc: c026/c027 added to the supersession list (found via plan-0 preflight); document: admitted as a stipulation scheme (the design's own worked re-homes are document-grounded). History re-typed by best fit, never split; dep re-points on a387/c047/c049/a007 recorded in their evidence or here.

claim: Code is not an abstraction layer that persists; it is the operational substrate - the only artifact in the system whose meaning is enforced by execution.

evidence.detail: Stated by the agent in the 2026-06-10 amieval discussion on anchored-answer schemas, against the claim that IDEs and code-reading become unnecessary as work migrates to NL agent discussion; the user directed verbatim conversion to a position and extraction into cb:. Grounded in a465's verification-not-generation principle: code is where enforcement lives, drift surfaces as a failing test.

claim: NL discussion above the substrate is productive exactly when every claim terminates in an anchor into the substrate; the anchor is what makes a prose claim adjudicable against the thing that runs.

evidence.detail: The position's central conditional, anchored to the mechanism that embodies it: CB.Codepath.resolve_anchor (lib/cb/codepath.ex) and the never-store-line-numbers rule (lib/cb/code_locator.ex). Both anchors verified resolving with exactly one grep -nF match at authoring time. Deps: the substrate claim (a484) plus a467's settled code-anchoring design.

claim: The editor's role shifts from authoring surface to adjudication surface - the place where claims meet the thing that actually runs.

evidence.detail: Anchored to the present-codepath skill's own instruction ('the reader clicks, the editor navigates', skills/present-codepath/SKILL.md); anchor verified resolving with exactly one match at authoring time. The shift follows from a484/a485: if claims must terminate in the substrate, the editor is where termination is checked by a human.

claim: Reading code re-scopes from building a whole mental model to reading the anchored stops that carry an argument.

evidence.detail: Anchored to the presenter rule that states the re-scoping ('the value is the reader reading the real file, not a wall of agent prose', skills/present-codepath/SKILL.md); anchor verified resolving with exactly one match at authoring time.

claim: Agent claims about code must carry code: anchors that resolve at read time; an unresolvable anchor is an ungrounded claim and must be fixed or withdrawn before the claim is presented.

evidence.detail: The prescriptive extraction of the position's conditional (a485), anchored to the resolver's enforcement site - the anchor-not-found warning constructor in lib/cb/codepath.ex; anchor verified resolving with exactly one match at authoring time. Recurring rule: fires on every agent answer that cites code; non-contract grade until a draft-mode verifier exists to machine-check compliance.
